mission.title: metallb: design: MetalLB CRD controller enhancement
mission.description:
This design document discusses implementing MetalLB Custom Resource definition"CRD"
as a mechanism to configure MetalLB's layer2 and BGP features instead of using ConfigMap.

ran mdl against the new md file
```
mdl 0001-metallb-crd.md 
```
Signed-off-by: Mohamed Mahmoud <mmahmoud@redhat.com>

Fixes https://github.com/metallb/metallb/issues/196.

resolution.summary:
## General

Fixes #529.

This PR adds a new feature to MetalLB called **peer autodiscovery**. This feature allows MetalLB to create BGP peers by discovering their configuration from annotations and labels on `Node` Kubernetes objects. The rationale and use case for this is explained in https://github.com/metallb/metallb/issues/529.

In addition to the main functionality, this PR adds a **status endpoint** to the speaker. This is an HTTP endpoint which allows querying the internal state of a speaker. The endpoint is available per protocol (BGP/Layer 2) at the following URI: `http://<speaker>:7473/status/<protocol>`.

While the endpoint is added generically at the "protocol" level, this PR implements a handler for BGP only.

Documentation as well as sample configuration for this feature are included with this PR.

Discovered peers (or "node peers") can coexist with "regular" peers - the user is free to use peer autodiscovery alone, in conjunction with static peer configuration or not use autodiscovery at all. The implementation ensures existing functionality isn't broken and filters duplicate peers in case a node peer that's identical to a static peer is discovered.

Finally, the user has full flexibility in determining exactly which BGP parameters should be discovered automatically as well as which annotations/labels to use for determining the values. The implementation is generic and doesn't expect a specific annotation/label format so as not to couple MetalLB to a specific i

Apply the fix from the linked pull request

git clone git@github.com:kinvolk/metallb.git
cd metallb
git checkout johananl/peer-autodiscovery

# Run the unit tests

# Deploy MetalLB to a local cluster
inv dev-env -p bgp
